_readme: Defines how sections relate to each other on a page. Use this after selecting sections from intents.json , check pairs_well_with to suggest complementary sections, avoid_with to flag conflicts, and page_roles to ensure layout coherence.

slideshow.notes: Strong visual opener. Should always be first in order when present. Don't follow it immediately with another full-width visual like media or countdown , break it up with a product or collections section first

banner.notes: Lightweight , works anywhere. Especially effective directly after slideshow or wrapping a countdown sale. Max 3 banner sections per page, space them out

special-product.notes: Spotlights one product. Avoid placing directly next to product-grid , it dilutes the spotlight. Best after slideshow or between collections and product-grid

countdown.notes: Urgency driver , place near the products it promotes. A banner above + countdown + product-grid below is a proven sale layout. Feels out of place next to informational sections like FAQs or blogs

collections.notes: Category browse , place early on the page, after the hero. Never use alongside all-collections (all-collections is for the list-collections template only)

product-grid.notes: Core selling section. Pair with testimonials below it to support purchase confidence. Avoid placing special-product directly adjacent , they compete visually

product-slider.notes: Good as a secondary product row after product-grid. Works well at the bottom of a page as a 'more to explore' section. Avoid next to collections , both are horizontal scrollers and create visual fatigue

multicolumn.notes: Features/benefits block. Works best in the middle or lower section of a page after commerce sections. Avoid placing next to blogs , both are card grids and look repetitive

testimonials.notes: Place after a product section to support purchase confidence. Never use alongside reviews on the same page , they serve the same purpose. testimonials = curated quotes (any page), reviews = platform reviews (product page only)

video-reviews.notes: UGC video proof. Most impactful on the product page above written reviews. Avoid pairing with media section , two video blocks on one page is too heavy

reviews.notes: Product page only. Always place after product-information. Dynamic , no manual content needed

faqs.notes: Objection handler , place near the bottom of the page after commerce and trust sections. Limit 1 per page. Feels disconnected next to urgency sections like countdown

text.notes: Flexible filler. Works anywhere but shines as an intro to multicolumn or before a map section

blogs.notes: Place at the bottom of the page , it drives users away to other pages. Avoid next to multicolumn (both are card grids) or testimonials (both are content rows)

link.notes: Lightweight CTA. Good as a social follow prompt at the bottom of the page or between content sections

media.notes: Full-width media embed. Don't stack with slideshow (both are dominant visuals) or video-reviews (two video sections is too heavy)

map.notes: Always near the bottom of the page, close to footer. Pairs naturally with a text section above it for address/contact details

special-offer.notes: Product page only. Renders as an off-screen drawer , placement in order has no visual effect on layout. Keep it in the template

all-collections.notes: list-collections template only. This section IS the page , don't add commerce or marketing sections alongside it

single-collection.notes: collection template only. Self-contained , handles its own search, sort, and pagination. Don't add other product sections

cart.notes: cart template only. Standalone , no other sections needed

page.notes: page template only. Auto-switches to contact form when page template = contact

_page_roles.anchor: Defines the visual identity of the page , usually first, large, and bold
_page_roles.accent: Supporting element , lightweight, can appear anywhere
_page_roles.focal: Draws attention to one specific thing
_page_roles.navigation: Helps users find where to go
_page_roles.trust: Builds confidence before or after a purchase decision

_recommended_homepage_order: countdown (urgency) , only if running a sale
